text.title: BenchmarkSuite — Standardized Task Evaluation
text.summary: BenchmarkSuite class for loading and running classification, QA, summarization, and code benchmarks with appropriate metrics per task type.

rule.statement: Select metrics appropriate to task type: exact_match for classification, contains_match + BLEU for QA, ROUGE for summarization.
rule.rationale: Applying BLEU to classification or exact_match to generation produces meaningless scores; metric-task mismatch is the most common benchmarking error.

rule.statement: Validate that loaded cases share a consistent schema before running benchmarks — check that expected_output is not None for more than 5% of cases.
rule.rationale: BenchmarkSuite does not validate loaded case schemas; mixing datasets with different field names silently produces empty expected_output and misleadingly high metrics.

rule.statement: Write a format adapter for each external dataset (SQuAD, TriviaQA, MMLU) that maps its schema to EvaluationCase(input, expected_output, metadata).
rule.rationale: BenchmarkSuite.load_qa_benchmark only supports a flat JSON format; real benchmark datasets require custom adapters.

section.title: Standard External Benchmarks
section.p: For production use, prefer established benchmarks over custom datasets for cross-model comparability.

list.item: MMLU — multi-task knowledge, accuracy metric, 57 subjects

rule.statement: Use lm-evaluation-harness for running standard benchmarks; do not reimplement MMLU or HellaSwag evaluation logic manually.
rule.rationale: Standard benchmark evaluation has subtle implementation details (few-shot formatting, answer normalization) that manual reimplementation gets wrong, producing non-comparable scores.
